skill.name: lambda-optimization-advisor
skill.description: Reviews AWS Lambda functions for performance, memory configuration, and cost optimization. Activates when users write Lambda handlers or discuss Lambda performance.
skill.summary: Reviews Rust AWS Lambda code for performance, cold start, memory, and cost improvements.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are Markdown examples, inline Rust and shell snippets, and example.com URLs, with no executable scripts, prompt injection, secret access, or data exfiltration. Risk is low because the community skill contains command examples but only requests read-only tools.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The cited locations are Markdown inline code or fenced examples that show Rust, TOML, or deployment commands for review guidance; the skill does not define executable scripts or request shell access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is entirely inside Markdown examples or inline references. The skill metadata allows only Read, Grep, and Glob, so these snippets are not directly executable by the skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are false positives. Both URLs use api.example.com inside illustrative Rust code that teaches client reuse; no instruction sends data to that endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domain is the reserved example.com documentation domain and appears in sample code only. No network-capable tool or exfiltration instruction is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert at the skill description and the system reconnaissance alert at the early-return example are false positives. No cryptographic API, host enumeration, system probing, or security-bypass instruction appears in the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain frontmatter describing Lambda optimization, and line 152 returns a validation error in sample Rust code. I found no semantic evidence of cryptography or reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Rust Lambda functions can waste time and money through sequential I/O, cold starts, and oversized binaries. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code review handlers and suggest targeted performance and cost improvements.
actual_capabilities: Identifies sequential async operations that could run concurrently.
Reviews client and resource initialization patterns for Lambda reuse.
Suggests Cargo release profile settings for smaller Rust binaries.
Recommends ARM64 builds for better Lambda price and performance.
Explains memory tuning tradeoffs for CPU-bound and I/O-bound workloads.
limitations: Focuses on Rust Lambda code and Cargo-based deployments.
Does not benchmark functions or query live AWS metrics.
Does not deploy, modify, or execute Lambda functions.
Provides guidance that should be validated with workload-specific tests.

use_cases.title: Reduce Cold Starts
use_cases.description: Review Rust Lambda handlers for binary size, architecture, and initialization patterns that affect cold start latency.

use_cases.title: Lower Lambda Cost
use_cases.description: Find opportunities to reduce billed duration through concurrency, batching, early returns, and memory tuning.

use_cases.title: Review Deployment Settings
use_cases.description: Check Cargo and cargo-lambda settings before release to improve runtime performance and package size.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust AWS Lambda handler for performance and cost issues. Focus on async operations, resource initialization, and memory guidance.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single Lambda handler

prompt_templates.title: Check Cold Start Risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Rust Lambda project for cold start risks. Review binary size settings, initialization work, client reuse, and ARM64 readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Memory and Duration
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this Lambda workload for memory tuning. Explain whether higher memory could reduce duration and total cost.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cost and runtime tuning for production workloads

prompt_templates.title: Audit Full Deployment Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Rust Lambda service from handler code through Cargo release settings and cargo-lambda deployment options. Provide prioritized optimization actions.

output_examples.input: A handler fetches user data, posts, and comments one after another.
output: Run independent requests concurrently to reduce billed duration.
Use a fallible join pattern when any failed request should fail the invocation.
Measure latency before and after because downstream rate limits may change the result.

output_examples.input: A function creates a new HTTP client inside every invocation.
output: Move the client into a container-level reusable resource.
Set explicit timeouts so failed upstream calls do not consume unnecessary billed time.
Keep request-specific data inside the handler to avoid cross-invocation state leaks.

output_examples.input: A project deploys only x86_64 builds with default release settings.
output: Consider ARM64 for better price and performance when dependencies support it.
Enable release settings that reduce binary size and improve cold start behavior.
Validate runtime behavior in staging before changing production architecture.

best_practices: Provide handler code, Cargo settings, and deployment memory values together.
Validate every recommendation with latency, error rate, and cost measurements.
Separate independent I/O work from dependent workflow steps before applying concurrency.
anti_patterns: Do not assume higher memory always lowers cost without measuring duration.
Do not create HTTP, database, or AWS SDK clients on every invocation.
Do not optimize binary size by removing diagnostics needed for production support.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy Lambda functions?
faq.answer: No. It reviews code and configuration guidance only.

faq.question: Is it specific to Rust?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples and recommendations are designed for Rust Lambda projects.

faq.question: Can it estimate exact AWS costs?
faq.answer: No. It can explain likely cost drivers, but exact cost requires real metrics.

faq.question: Does it need AWS credentials?
faq.answer: No. The skill only needs project files that you choose to share.

faq.question: Can it help with cold starts?
faq.answer: Yes. It reviews binary size, architecture, initialization, and reusable resources.

faq.question: Should I apply every recommendation?
faq.answer: No. Test recommendations against your workload, latency goals, and operational requirements.
